zh.lifeSummary: 當Nicholas de Stafford於1246年7月12日出生在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾時，他的父親Robert de Stafford30歲，母親Alice de Corbet21歲。他於1270年與Eleanor de Clinton在英國斯塔福爾斯塔福德結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。他去世於1287年8月1日在威爾士卡馬特史爾德雷斯爾享年41歲，埋葬在英國斯塔福爾斯塔福德。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Stafford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name principally from Stafford (Staffordshire), but occasionally from other places with similar names, such as Stafford House in Ifield (Sussex), possibly East and West Stowford in East Down (Devon), and three minor places in Devon called Stafford, in Dolton, Broadhembury, and Colyton parishes. The places are all named with Old English ford ‘ford’ as the final element, but have different initial elements. The Staffordshire placename has Old English stæth ‘river bank, shore’; Stafford in Colyton (Devon) has Old English stān ‘stone, rock’; the Sussex placename has Old English stēor ‘steer, bullock’; East and West Stowford (Devon) have Old English stæf ‘staff, stave, rod’; Stafford in Dolton and Stafford in Broadhembury (Devon) may have Old English stæth, stān, or stæf.

variants.name: Stocker
meanings: German and Swiss German (also Stöcker): topographic name for someone living by a tree stump (see Stock 3) or an occupational name for a tree cutter, from Middle High German stocken ‘to clear land’.
German and Swiss German (also Stöcker); Dutch: occupational name from Middle High German, Middle Dutch stocker ‘jailer’.

English: occupational name from Middle English stokker ‘one who sells stockfish’ (fish dried in the air without salt). This was the usual source of the name in medieval London, where a bylaw of 1419 stated that no stokker should board a ship to buy fish (presumably in order to forestall the market).

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Nicholas de Stafford was born on 12 July 1246, in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Robert de Stafford, was 30 and his mother, Alice de Corbet, was 21. He married Eleanor de Clinton in 1270, in Stafford, Staffordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. He died on 1 August 1287, in Dryslwyn, Carmarthenshire, Wales, at the age of 41, and was buried in Stafford, Staffordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Nicholas de Stafford est né le 12 juillet 1246, à Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert de Stafford, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Alice de Corbet, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Eleanor de Clinton en 1270, à Stafford, Staffordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Il est décédé le 1 août 1287, à Dryslwyn, Carmarthenshire, Galles, à l’âge de 41 ans, et a été enterré à Stafford, Staffordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Nicholas de Stafford am 12. Juli 1246 in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert de Stafford 30 und seine Mutter Alice de Corbet 21. Er hat 1270 in Stafford, Staffordshire, England Eleanor de Clinton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Er starb am 1. August 1287 im Alter von 41 Jahren in Dryslwyn, Carmarthenshire, Wales und wurde in Stafford, Staffordshire, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Staffordが1246年7月12日にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアで生まれた時，父Robert de Staffordは30歳，母Alice de Corbetは21歳であった。1270年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアスタフォードでEleanor de Clintonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼は1287年8月1日にウェールズカーマースエンシュアイアドリスルウインで41歳で死去し，イギリススタフォードシュアイアスタフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Stafford(이)가 1246년 7월 12일 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert de Stafford(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice de Corbet(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1270년 영국 스태퍼드실르 스태퍼드에서 Eleanor de Clinton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1287년 8월 1일 웨일스 카마테실르 드리스윈에서 41세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스태퍼드실르 스태퍼드에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Eleanor de Clinton em 1270, em Stafford, Staffordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1 de agosto de 1287, em Dryslwyn, Carmarthenshire, Gales, com 41 anos, e foi sepultado em Stafford, Staffordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Nicholas de Stafford родился 12 июля 1246 г. в местности стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Robert de Stafford было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice de Corbet, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Eleanor de Clinton в 1270 г. в местности стаффорд, стаффордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Он умер 1 августа 1287 г. в местности дрислвин, кармартеншир, Уэльс в возрасте 41 год и был похоронен в местности стаффорд, стаффордшир, Англия.
